sanskrit_devanagari:
यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते ।
सर्वसङ्कल्पसन्न्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते ॥ ४ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
yadā hi nendriyārtheṣu na karmasv anuṣajjate |
sarva-saṅkalpa-sannyāsī yogārūḍhas tadocyate || 4 ||

anvaya.meaning: in the objects of the senses

translations.verbatim_quote: A person is said to be elevated in yoga when, having renounced all material desires, he neither acts for sense gratification nor engages in fruitive activities.
A person is said to be elevated in yoga when, having renounced all material desires, he neither acts for sense gratification nor engages in fruitive activities.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 664 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: When a person is fully engaged in the transcendental loving service of the Lord, he is pleased in himself, and thus he is no longer engaged in sense gratification or in fruitive activities. Otherwise, one must be engaged in sense gratification, since one cannot live without engagement.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 6.4 Hi, verily; yada, when; a yogi who is concentrating his mind, sarva-sankalpa-sannyasi, who has given up thought about everything-who is apt to give up (sannyasa) all (sarva) thoughts (sankalpa) which are the causes of desire, for things here and hereafter; na anusajjate, does not becom…
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-6-4-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: 6.4 When indeed not in the objects of the senses nor in actions one is attached, then, having renounced all motives (sankalpas), the cause of all karmas, one is said to be elevated in yoga.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 6.4 When this Yogin, because of his natural disposition to the experience of the self, loses attachment, i.e., gets detached from sense-objects, i.e., things other than the self, and actions associated with them — then he has abandoned all desires and is said to have climbed the heights of…
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-6-4-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: 6.4 He becomes 'ascended in yoga' when there is no attachment to objects of the senses (sound etc.) and no attachment to actions performed for the sake of those objects. Having renounced every motive (saṅkalpa) — both the desires and the imaginings that produce them — that one is yogārūḍha.

positions.rendering: established/elevated in yoga, having renounced all motives (sankalpas), the cause of all karmas
ascended in yoga; defined operationally by detachment from sense-objects, actions, and saṅkalpa
elevated in yoga, having renounced all material desires

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: Translators agree on the diagnostic — detachment from sense-objects, from actions, and from all saṅkalpas. The metaphysical reach of yogārūḍha (Advaita: ready for jñāna-niṣṭhā; Vishishtadvaita: prepared for sustained Self-vision) differs by tradition.

literal_meaning: When one is no longer attached to sense-objects nor to actions — having renounced all fancies — one is said to be ascended in yoga.
traditional_meaning_consensus: Krishna defines the diagnostic of yogārūḍha (ascended in yoga). The marks: detachment from sense-objects, detachment from actions themselves, renunciation of all saṅkalpas. The verse closes the four-verse opening definition: 6.1 redefined renunciation; 6.2 anchored the cut at saṅkalpa; 6.3 staged the practice; 6.4 names the destination. The internal sequence is doctrinally complete.
